user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Fabrikam Residences. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 14:05 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences consumption trend

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 08:11 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 11:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences consumption trend

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-25
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:01:30] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into consumption trend.

[00:08:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:22:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:34:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:38:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:43:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.
